votes.rationale: P9-PII-1 round 2 fix is appropriate and complete on branch harden/pass-9. (1) Zero genuine leaks: git grep -i over transcripts/ returns 0 for both '[REDACTED]' and '[REDACTED]', and a broad '[a-z0-9-]+\.local' scan of the corpus is empty. Critically, the HEAD commit (22cbcad) re-captured 39 files / 792 insertions including the active session WITH the env live, and the count stayed at 0 — demonstrating the scrub actually fires on new captures, not merely that an old leak was one-time-cleaned. (2) Bare 'hc-grc' preserved at 1446 files (the '~1400' project token), confirming no over-redaction of legitimate paths/repo refs. (3) The pre-commit hook exports ASSAY_SCRUB_HOSTS (default 'hc-grc', honoring an external value via ${VAR:-default}) on line 15 BEFORE invoking capture_transcripts.py on line 16. (4) Scrubber logic is correct: machine hostnames (auto-derived via socket/platform/scutil) redact bare+.local; ASSAY_SCRUB_HOSTS hosts redact ONLY .local. Both named tests pass and discriminate: test_precommit_hook parses split('capture_transcripts.py')[0] so it fails if the export moves after capture (verified via simulation); test_scrub forces a deterministic machine hostname and asserts machine bare+.local redaction, namespace .local-only redaction, bare-token preservation, and that unrelated settings.local.json is untouched (guards against a blanket .local rule). The vector is durable because the hook sets the env on every commit and the capture path reads it. The operator-hostname PII vector is now actually closed and durable against future captures.
concerns: Non-blocking observation (not a defect in this fix): _HOSTNAME_RES is computed at module import (capture_transcripts.py:251) from the then-current env, so the hook's shell-level export-before-process is load-bearing. Any future caller that invokes the scrubber without exporting ASSAY_SCRUB_HOSTS would silently skip the namespace .local rule (the machine-hostname rule is unconditional/auto-derived and remains robust). The committed pre-commit hook is the only capture entrypoint and sets it correctly, so the closure holds for the actual workflow.

votes.rationale: P9-PII-1 round 2 fix verified on branch harden/pass-9. All four criteria pass. (1) ZERO leaks: `grep -ri 'hc-grc\.local'` and `grep -ri '[REDACTED]'` over transcripts/ both return 0 — and critically, HEAD (22cbcad) just re-captured the active session plus ~15 transcript files WITH the env set, and none of those re-captured files contain either token, proving the corpus stayed clean through a real capture. (2) Bare 'hc-grc' preserved across 1446 files (matches the ~1400 figure; raw occurrences are higher but the project token is intentionally not over-redacted). (3) The hook (.githooks/pre-commit) does `export ASSAY_SCRUB_HOSTS="${ASSAY_SCRUB_HOSTS:-hc-grc}"` BEFORE invoking scripts/capture_transcripts.py, and honors an externally-set value via the :- default. (4) Both tests pass and discriminate under mutation: removing the export line makes test_precommit_hook_wires_namespace_host_into_capture FAIL (AssertionError at line 225); and I reproduced the exact root-cause mechanism — with ASSAY_SCRUB_HOSTS unset, the scrubber leaves 'hc-proj.local' (the [REDACTED] analog) unredacted, while the test sets the env and asserts the .local form is gone, the bare token preserved, and unrelated settings.local.json untouched. Implementation matches the described contract: _hostname_patterns() redacts the auto-derived machine hostname bare+.local and an ASSAY_SCRUB_HOSTS host's .local form only. Durability confirmed: git core.hooksPath=.githooks and the hook is executable (rwxr-xr-x), so future commits on the production node run it with the env exported. The operator-hostname PII vector is closed and durable against future captures.
concerns: Durability is contingent on git core.hooksPath=.githooks being configured locally — it is on this production node, but a fresh clone without that config would skip the hook and re-introduce [REDACTED]. This is a generic hook-installation property, not specific to this fix, and no repo-side setup script wiring hooksPath was found; acceptable for the dedicated capture node but worth a one-line bootstrap if the workflow ever runs from a fresh clone.

_HOSTNAME_RES is computed once at module import, so the env must be set before the process starts (the hook does this correctly via export); any non-hook caller of the scrubber that sets the env after import would not pick up the rule. Not a leak in the committed corpus, just an invariant to preserve.

summary: Pass-9: re-confirm P9-PII-1 hostname remediation after the hook-wiring fix
